Studydirected Comptroller General of United States to conduct a study of the certification program authorized by this section to determine if the program has resulted in improvement of quality and accessibility of mammography services, and if the program has reduced the frequency of poor quality mammography and improved early detection of breast cancer, with Comptroller General, not later than 3 years from , submit to Congress an interim report of results of study and, not later than 5 years from such date to submit a final report.Section 3 of Pub. Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–183, title VII, § 707107 Stat. 2241Dec. 14, 1993“The Secretary of Health and Human Services is authorized to issue interim final regulations—“(1)under which the Secretary may approve accreditation bodies under section 354(e) of the Public Health Service Act (); and42 U.S.C. 263b(e)“(2)establishing quality standards under section 354(f) of the Public Health Service Act ().”42 U.S.C. 263b(f)

children.heading: Accreditation body
children.label: 1 Accreditation body
children.text: The term “accreditation body” means a body that has been approved by the Secretary under subsection (e)(1)(A) to accredit mammography facilities.

children.heading: Certificate
children.label: 2 Certificate
children.text: The term “certificate” means the certificate described in subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “facility” means a hospital, outpatient department, clinic, radiology practice, or mobile unit, an office of a physician, or other facility as determined by the Secretary, that conducts breast cancer screening or diagnosis through mammography activities. Such term does not include a facility of the Department of Veterans Affairs.

children.heading: Activities
children.label: B Activities
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the activities of a facility include the operation of equipment to produce the mammogram, the processing of the film, the initial interpretation of the mammogram and the viewing conditions for that interpretation. Where procedures such as the film processing, or the interpretation of the mammogram are performed in a location different from where the mammogram is performed, the facility performing the mammogram shall be responsible for meeting the quality standards described in subsection (f).

children.heading: Inspection
children.label: 4 Inspection
children.text: The term “inspection” means an onsite evaluation of the facility by the Secretary, or State or local agency on behalf of the Secretary.

children.heading: Mammogram
children.label: 5 Mammogram
children.text: The term “mammogram” means a radiographic image produced through mammography.

children.heading: Mammography
children.label: 6 Mammography
children.text: The term “mammography” means radiography of the breast.

children.heading: Survey
children.label: 7 Survey
children.text: The term “survey” means an onsite physics consultation and evaluation performed by a medical physicist as described in subsection (f)(1)(E).

children.heading: Review physician
children.label: 8 Review physician
children.text: The term “review physician” means a physician as prescribed by the Secretary under subsection (f)(1)(D) who meets such additional requirements as may be established by an accreditation body under subsection (e) and approved by the Secretary to review clinical images under subsection (e)(1)(B)(i) on behalf of the accreditation body.

children.label: i
children.text: that is issued, and, if applicable, renewed, by the Secretary in accordance with paragraphs (1) or (2) of subsection (c);1So in original. Probably should be “paragraph”.1

children.label: ii
children.text: that is applicable to the examination or procedure to be conducted; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that is displayed prominently in such facility; or

children.label: i
children.text: that is issued by the Secretary in accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4) of subsection (c);

children.label: ii
children.text: that is applicable to the examination or procedure to be conducted; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that is displayed prominently in such facility.

children.label: A
children.text: operate radiological equipment that is used to image the breast;

children.label: B
children.text: provide for the interpretation of a mammogram produced by such equipment at the facility or under arrangements with a qualified individual at a facility different from where the mammography examination is performed; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide for the processing of film produced by such equipment at the facility or under arrangements with a qualified individual at a facility different from where the mammography examination is performed.

children.heading: Issuance and renewal of certificates
children.label: c Issuance and renewal of certificates

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may issue or renew a certificate for a facility if the person or agent described in subsection (d)(1)(A) meets the applicable requirements of subsection (d)(1) with respect to the facility. The Secretary may issue or renew a certificate under this paragraph for not more than 3 years.

children.label: A
children.text: The facility has submitted the required materials to the accreditation body within the established time frames for the submission of such materials but the accreditation body is unable to complete the reaccreditation process before the certification expires.

children.label: B
children.text: The facility has acquired additional or replacement equipment, or has had significant personnel changes or other unforeseen situations that have caused the facility to be unable to meet reaccreditation timeframes, but in the opinion of the accreditation body have not compromised the quality of mammography.

children.heading: Limited provisional certificate
children.label: 3 Limited provisional certificate
children.text: The Secretary may, upon the request of an accreditation body, issue a limited provisional certificate to an entity to enable the entity to conduct examinations for educational purposes while an onsite visit from an accreditation body is in progress. Such certificate shall be valid only during the time the site visit team from the accreditation body is physically in the facility, and in no case shall be valid for longer than 72 hours. The issuance of a certificate under this paragraph, shall not preclude the entity from qualifying for a provisional certificate under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Provisional certificate
children.label: 4 Provisional certificate
children.text: The Secretary may issue a provisional certificate for an entity to enable the entity to qualify as a facility. The applicant for a provisional certificate shall meet the requirements of subsection (d)(1), except providing information required by clauses (iii) and (iv) of subsection (d)(1)(A). A provisional certificate may be in effect no longer than 6 months from the date it is issued, except that it may be extended once for a period of not more than 90 days if the owner, lessor, or agent of the facility demonstrates to the Secretary that without such extension access to mammography in the geographic area served by the facility would be significantly reduced and if the owner, lessor, or agent of the facility will describe in a report to the Secretary steps that will be taken to qualify the facility for certification under subsection (b)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the manufacturer, model, and type of each x-ray machine, image receptor, and processor operated in the performance of mammography by the facility;

children.label: I
children.text: the types of procedures performed and the number of such procedures performed in the prior 12 months;

children.label: II
children.text: the methodologies for mammography; and

children.label: III
children.text: the names and qualifications (educational background, training, and experience) of the personnel performing mammography and the physicians reading and interpreting the results from the procedures;

children.label: iii
children.text: proof of on-site survey by a qualified medical physicist as described in subsection (f)(1)(E); and

children.label: iv
children.text: proof of accreditation in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe; and

children.label: i
children.text: a satisfactory assurance that the facility will be operated in accordance with standards established by the Secretary under subsection (f) to assure the safety and accuracy of mammography;

children.label: I
children.text: permit inspections under subsection (g);

children.label: II
children.text: make such records and information available, and submit such reports, to the Secretary as the Secretary may require; and

children.label: III
children.text: update the information submitted under subparagraph (A) or assurances submitted under this subparagraph on a timely basis as required by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: a statement of the grounds on which the denial is based, and

children.label: B
children.text: an opportunity for an appeal in accordance with the procedures set forth in regulations of the Secretary published at part 498 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Effect of denial
children.label: 3 Effect of denial
children.text: If the application for the certification of a facility is denied, the facility may not operate unless the denial of the application is overturned at the conclusion of the administrative appeals process provided in the regulations referred to in paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may approve a private nonprofit organization or State agency to accredit facilities for purposes of subsection (d)(1)(A)(iv) if the accreditation body meets the standards for accreditation established by the Secretary as described in subparagraph (B) and provides the assurances required by subparagraph (C).

children.label: I
children.text: a review of clinical images from each facility accredited by such body not less often than every 3 years which review will be made by qualified review physicians; and

children.label: II
children.text: a review of a random sample of clinical images from such facilities in each 3-year period beginning , which review will be made by qualified review physicians;October 1, 1994

children.label: ii
children.text: standards that prohibit individuals conducting the reviews described in clause (i) from maintaining any relationship to the facility undergoing review which would constitute a conflict of interest;

children.label: iii
children.text: standards that limit the imposition of fees for accreditation to reasonable amounts;

children.label: iv
children.text: standards that require as a condition of accreditation that each facility undergo a survey at least annually by a medical physicist as described in subsection (f)(1)(E) to ensure that the facility meets the standards described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (f)(1);

children.label: v
children.text: standards that require monitoring and evaluation of such survey, as prescribed by the Secretary;

children.label: vi
children.text: standards that are equal to standards established under subsection (f) which are relevant to accreditation as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: vii
children.text: such additional standards as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: comply with the standards as described in subparagraph (B);

children.label: ii
children.text: comply with the requirements described in paragraph (4);

children.label: iii
children.text: submit to the Secretary the name of any facility for which the accreditation body denies, suspends, or revokes accreditation;

children.label: iv
children.text: notify the Secretary in a timely manner before the accreditation body changes the standards of the body;

children.label: v
children.text: notify each facility accredited by the accreditation body if the Secretary withdraws approval of the accreditation body under paragraph (2) in a timely manner; and

children.label: vi
children.text: provide such other additional information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: D Regulations
children.text: Not later than 9 months after , the Secretary shall promulgate regulations under which the Secretary may approve an accreditation body.October 27, 1992

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations under which the Secretary may withdraw the approval of an accreditation body if the Secretary determines that the accreditation body does not meet the standards under subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1), the requirements of clauses (i) through (vi) of subparagraph (C) of paragraph (1), or the requirements of paragraph (4).

children.heading: Effect of withdrawal
children.label: B Effect of withdrawal
children.text: If the Secretary withdraws the approval of an accreditation body under subparagraph (A), the certificate of any facility accredited by the body shall continue in effect until the expiration of a reasonable period, as determined by the Secretary, for such facility to obtain another accreditation.

children.label: A
children.text: the standards described in paragraph (1)(B) which the Secretary determines are applicable to the facility, and

children.label: B
children.text: such other standards which the accreditation body may require.

children.label: A
children.text: make onsite visits on an annual basis of a sufficient number of the facilities accredited by the body to allow a reasonable estimate of the performance of the body; and

children.label: B
children.text: take such additional measures as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Revocation of accreditation
children.label: 5 Revocation of accreditation
children.text: If an accreditation body revokes the accreditation of a facility, the certificate of the facility shall continue in effect until such time as may be determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: inspecting under subsection (g)(2) a sufficient number of the facilities accredited by the body to allow a reasonable estimate of the performance of the body; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such additional means as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: The Secretary shall annually prepare and submit to the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report that describes the results of the evaluation conducted in accordance with subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: standards that require establishment and maintenance of a quality assurance and quality control program at each facility that is adequate and appropriate to ensure the reliability, clarity, and accuracy of interpretation of mammograms and standards for appropriate radiation dose;

children.label: B
children.text: standards that require use of radiological equipment specifically designed for mammography, including radiologic standards and standards for other equipment and materials used in conjunction with such equipment;

children.label: I
children.text: be licensed by a State to perform radiological procedures; or

children.label: II
children.text: be certified as qualified to perform radiological procedures by an organization described in paragraph (2)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 2-year period beginning , meet training standards for personnel who perform mammography or meet experience requirements which shall at a minimum include 1 year of experience in the performance of mammography; andOctober 1, 1994

children.label: iii
children.text: upon the expiration of such 2-year period meet minimum training standards for personnel who perform mammograms;

children.label: I
children.text: by a board described in paragraph (2)(B); or

children.label: II
children.text: by a program that complies with the standards described in paragraph (2)(C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: who meets training and continuing medical education requirements as established by the Secretary;

children.label: i
children.text: licensed or approved by a State to perform such surveys, reviews, or inspections for mammography facilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: certified in diagnostic radiological physics or certified as qualified to perform such surveys by a board as described in paragraph (2)(D); or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the first 5 years after , who meet other criteria established by the Secretary which are comparable to the criteria described in clause (i) or (ii);October 27, 1992

children.label: F
children.text: a requirement that a medical physicist who is qualified in mammography as described in subparagraph (E) survey mammography equipment and oversee quality assurance practices at each facility;

children.label: I
children.text: except as provided in subclause (II), maintain the mammogram in the permanent medical records of the patient for a period of not less than 5 years, or not less than 10 years if no subsequent mammograms of such patient are performed at the facility, or longer if mandated by State law; and

children.label: II
children.text: upon the request of or on behalf of the patient, transfer the mammogram to a medical institution, to a physician of the patient, or to the patient directly; and

children.label: I
children.text: a facility must assure the preparation of a written report of the results of any mammography examination signed by the interpreting physician;

children.label: II
children.text: such written report shall be provided to the patient’s physicians (if any);

children.label: III
children.text: if such a physician is not available or if there is no such physician, the written report shall be sent directly to the patient; and

children.label: IV
children.text: whether or not such a physician is available or there is no such physician, a summary of the written report shall be sent directly to the patient in terms easily understood by a lay person; and

children.label: H
children.text: standards relating to special techniques for mammography of patients with breast implants.

children.label: A
children.text: specify organizations eligible to certify individuals to perform radiological procedures as required by paragraph (1)(C);

children.label: B
children.text: specify boards eligible to certify physicians to interpret radiological procedures, including mammography, as required by paragraph (1)(D);

children.label: C
children.text: establish standards for a program to certify physicians described in paragraph (1)(D); and

children.label: D
children.text: specify boards eligible to certify medical physicists who are qualified to survey mammography equipment and to oversee quality assurance practices at mammography facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter and inspect facilities to determine compliance with the certification requirements under subsection (b) and the standards established under subsection (f). The Secretary shall, if feasible, delegate to a State or local agency the authority to make such inspections.

children.heading: Identification
children.label: B Identification
children.text: The Secretary, or State or local agency acting on behalf of the Secretary, may conduct inspections only on presenting identification to the owner, operator, or agent in charge of the facility to be inspected.

children.label: i
children.text: shall have access to all equipment, materials, records, and information that the Secretary or State or local agency considers necessary to determine whether the facility is being operated in accordance with this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may copy, or require the facility to submit to the Secretary or the State or local agency, any of the materials, records, or information.

children.heading: Qualifications of inspectors
children.label: D Qualifications of inspectors
children.text: Qualified individuals, as determined by the Secretary, shall conduct all inspections. The Secretary may request that a State or local agency acting on behalf of the Secretary designate a qualified officer or employee to conduct the inspections, or designate a qualified Federal officer or employee to conduct inspections. The Secretary shall establish minimum qualifications and appropriate training for inspectors and criteria for certification of inspectors in order to inspect facilities for compliance with subsection (f).

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: E Frequency
children.text: The Secretary or State or local agency acting on behalf of the Secretary shall conduct inspections under this paragraph of each facility not less often than annually, subject to paragraph (6).

children.heading: Records and annual reports
children.label: F Records and annual reports
children.text: The Secretary or a State or local agency acting on behalf of the Secretary which is responsible for inspecting mammography facilities shall maintain records of annual inspections required under this paragraph for a period as prescribed by the Secretary. Such a State or local agency shall annually prepare and submit to the Secretary a report concerning the inspections carried out under this paragraph. Such reports shall include a description of the facilities inspected and the results of such inspections.

children.heading: Inspection of accredited facilities
children.label: 2 Inspection of accredited facilities
children.text: The Secretary shall inspect annually a sufficient number of the facilities accredited by an accreditation body to provide the Secretary with a reasonable estimate of the performance of such body.

children.heading: Inspection of facilities inspected by State or local agencies
children.label: 3 Inspection of facilities inspected by State or local agencies
children.text: The Secretary shall inspect annually facilities inspected by State or local agencies acting on behalf of the Secretary to assure a reasonable performance by such State or local agencies.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 4 Timing
children.text: The Secretary, or State or local agency, may conduct inspections under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3), during regular business hours or at a mutually agreeable time and after providing such notice as the Secretary may prescribe, except that the Secretary may waive such requirements if the continued performance of mammography at such facility threatens the public health.

children.heading: Limited reinspection
children.label: 5 Limited reinspection
children.text: Nothing in this section limits the authority of the Secretary to conduct limited reinspections of facilities found not to be in compliance with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may establish a demonstration program under which inspections under paragraph (1) of selected facilities are conducted less frequently by the Secretary (or as applicable, by State or local agencies acting on behalf of the Secretary) than the interval specified in subparagraph (E) of such paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: The program may not be implemented before . Preparations for the program may be carried out prior to such date.April 1, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: In carrying out the program, the Secretary may not select a facility for inclusion in the program unless the facility is substantially free of incidents of noncompliance with the standards under subsection (f). The Secretary may at any time provide that a facility will no longer be included in the program.

children.label: iii
children.text: The number of facilities selected for inclusion in the program shall be sufficient to provide a statistically significant sample, subject to compliance with clause (ii).

children.label: iv
children.text: Facilities that are selected for inclusion in the program shall be inspected at such intervals as the Secretary determines will reasonably ensure that the facilities are maintaining compliance with such standards.

children.label: A
children.text: Directed plans of correction which afford a facility an opportunity to correct violations in a timely manner.

children.label: B
children.text: Payment for the cost of onsite monitoring.

children.heading: Patient information
children.label: 2 Patient information
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the quality of mammography performed by a facility (whether or not certified pursuant to subsection (c)) was so inconsistent with the quality standards established pursuant to subsection (f) as to present a significant risk to individual or public health, the Secretary may require such facility to notify patients who received mammograms at such facility, and their referring physicians, of the deficiencies presenting such risk, the potential harm resulting, appropriate remedial measures, and such other relevant information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: failure to obtain a certificate as required by subsection (b),

children.label: B
children.text: each failure by a facility to substantially comply with, or each day on which a facility fails to substantially comply with, the standards established under subsection (f) or the requirements described in subclauses (I) through (III) of subsection (d)(1)(B)(ii),

children.label: C
children.text: each failure to notify a patient of risk as required by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (2), and

children.label: D
children.text: each violation, or for each aiding and abetting in a violation of, any provision of, or regulation promulgated under, this section by an owner, operator, or any employee of a facility required to have a certificate.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: 4 Procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement procedures with respect to when and how each of the sanctions is to be imposed under paragraphs (1) through (3). Such procedures shall provide for notice to the owner or operator of the facility and a reasonable opportunity for the owner or operator to respond to the proposed sanctions and appropriate procedures for appealing determinations relating to the imposition of sanctions.

children.label: A
children.text: has been guilty of misrepresentation in obtaining the certificate;

children.label: B
children.text: has failed to comply with the requirements of subsection (d)(1)(B)(ii)(III) or the standards established by the Secretary under subsection (f);

children.label: C
children.text: has failed to comply with reasonable requests of the Secretary (or of an accreditation body approved pursuant to subsection (e)) for any record, information, report, or material that the Secretary (or such accreditation body or State carrying out certification program requirements pursuant to subsection (q)) concludes is necessary to determine the continued eligibility of the facility for a certificate or continued compliance with the standards established under subsection (f);

children.label: D
children.text: has refused a reasonable request of the Secretary, any Federal officer or employee duly designated by the Secretary, or any State or local officer or employee duly designated by the State or local agency, for permission to inspect the facility or the operations and pertinent records of the facility in accordance with subsection (g);

children.label: E
children.text: has violated or aided and abetted in the violation of any provision of, or regulation promulgated under, this section; or

children.label: F
children.text: has failed to comply with a sanction imposed under subsection (h).

children.label: i
children.text: the failure or violation was intentional; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the failure or violation presents a serious risk to human health.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: B Hearing
children.text: If the Secretary suspends a certificate under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall provide an opportunity for a hearing to the owner or operator of the facility not later than 60 days from the effective date of the suspension. The suspension shall remain in effect until the decision of the Secretary made after the hearing.

children.heading: Ineligibility to own or operate facilities after revocation
children.label: 3 Ineligibility to own or operate facilities after revocation
children.text: If the Secretary revokes the certificate of a facility on the basis of an act described in paragraph (1), no person who owned or operated the facility at the time of the act may, within 2 years of the revocation of the certificate, own or operate a facility that requires a certificate under this section.

continuation of any activity related to the provision of mammography by a facility would constitute a serious risk to human health, the Secretary may bring suit in the district court of the United States for the district in which the facility is situated to enjoin continuation of the activity; and

a facility is operating without a certificate as required by subsection (b), the Secretary may bring suit in the district court of the United States for the district in which the facility is situated to enjoin the operation of the facility.

children.heading: Petition
children.label: 1 Petition
children.text: If the Secretary imposes a sanction on a facility under subsection (h) or suspends or revokes the certificate of a facility under subsection (i), the owner or operator of the facility may, not later than 60 days after the date the action of the Secretary becomes final, file a petition with the United States court of appeals for the circuit in which the facility is situated for judicial review of the action. As soon as practicable after receipt of the petition, the clerk of the court shall transmit a copy of the petition to the Secretary or other officer designated by the Secretary. As soon as practicable after receipt of the copy, the Secretary shall file in the court the record on which the action of the Secretary is based, as provided in .section 2112 of title 28

children.heading: Additional evidence
children.label: 2 Additional evidence
children.text: If the petitioner applies to the court for leave to adduce additional evidence, and shows to the satisfaction of the court that the additional evidence is material and that there were reasonable grounds for the failure to adduce such evidence in the proceeding before the Secretary, the court may order the additional evidence (and evidence in rebuttal of the additional evidence) to be taken before the Secretary, and to be adduced upon the hearing in such manner and upon such terms and conditions as the court may determine to be proper. The Secretary may modify the findings of the Secretary as to the facts, or make new findings, by reason of the additional evidence so taken, and the Secretary shall file the modified or new findings, and the recommendations of the Secretary, if any, for the modification or setting aside of the original action of the Secretary with the return of the additional evidence.

children.heading: Judgment of court
children.label: 3 Judgment of court
children.text: Upon the filing of the petition referred to in paragraph (1), the court shall have jurisdiction to affirm the action, or to set the action aside in whole or in part, temporarily or permanently. The findings of the Secretary as to the facts, if supported by substantial evidence, shall be conclusive.

children.heading: Finality of judgment
children.label: 4 Finality of judgment
children.text: The judgment of the court affirming or setting aside, in whole or in part, any action of the Secretary shall be final, subject to review by the Supreme Court of the United States upon certiorari or certification, as provided in .section 1254 of title 28

children.label: A
children.text: that have been convicted under Federal or State laws relating to fraud and abuse, false billings, or kickbacks;

children.label: B
children.text: that have been subject to sanctions under subsection (h), together with a statement of the reasons for the sanctions;

children.label: C
children.text: that have had certificates revoked or suspended under subsection (i), together with a statement of the reasons for the revocation or suspension;

children.label: D
children.text: against which the Secretary has taken action under subsection (j), together with a statement of the reasons for the action;

children.label: E
children.text: whose accreditation has been revoked, together with a statement of the reasons of the revocation;

children.label: F
children.text: against which a State has taken adverse action; and

children.label: G
children.text: that meets such other measures of performance as the Secretary may develop.

children.heading: Date
children.label: 2 Date
children.text: The information to be compiled under paragraph (1) shall be information for the calendar year preceding the date the information is to be made available to the public.

children.heading: Explanatory information
children.label: 3 Explanatory information
children.text: The information to be compiled under paragraph (1) shall be accompanied by such explanatory information as may be appropriate to assist in the interpretation of the information compiled under such paragraph.

children.heading: State laws
children.label: m State laws
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of any State to enact and enforce laws relating to the matters covered by this section that are at least as stringent as this section or the regulations issued under this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall establish an advisory committee to be known as the National Mammography Quality Assurance Advisory Committee (hereafter in this subsection referred to as the “Advisory Committee”).

children.label: A
children.text: physicians,

children.label: B
children.text: practitioners, and

children.label: C
children.text: other health professionals,

children.label: A
children.text: advise the Secretary on appropriate quality standards and regulations for mammography facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: advise the Secretary on appropriate standards and regulations for accreditation bodies;

children.label: C
children.text: advise the Secretary in the development of regulations with respect to sanctions;

children.label: D
children.text: assist in developing procedures for monitoring compliance with standards under subsection (f);

children.label: E
children.text: make recommendations and assist in the establishment of a mechanism to investigate consumer complaints;

children.label: F
children.text: report on new developments concerning breast imaging that should be considered in the oversight of mammography facilities;

children.label: G
children.text: determine whether there exists a shortage of mammography facilities in rural and health professional shortage areas and determine the effects of personnel or other requirements of subsection (f) on access to the services of such facilities in such areas;

children.label: H
children.text: determine whether there will exist a sufficient number of medical physicists after , to assure compliance with the requirements of subsection (f)(1)(E);October 1, 1999

children.label: I
children.text: determine the costs and benefits of compliance with the requirements of this section (including the requirements of regulations promulgated under this section); and

children.label: J
children.text: perform other activities that the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 4 Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall meet not less than quarterly for the first 3 years of the program and thereafter, at least annually.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 5 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint a chairperson of the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: o Consultations
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall consult with appropriate Federal agencies within the Department of Health and Human Services for the purposes of developing standards, regulations, evaluations, and procedures for compliance and oversight.

children.heading: Breast cancer screening surveillance research grants
children.label: p Breast cancer screening surveillance research grants

children.heading: Grants
children.label: A Grants
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants to such entities as the Secretary may determine to be appropriate to establish surveillance systems in selected geographic areas to provide data to evaluate the functioning and effectiveness of breast cancer screening programs in the United States, including assessments of participation rates in screening mammography, diagnostic procedures, incidence of breast cancer, mode of detection (mammography screening or other methods), outcome and follow up information, and such related epidemiologic analyses that may improve early cancer detection and contribute to reduction in breast cancer mortality. Grants may be awarded for further research on breast cancer surveillance systems upon the Secretary’s review of the evaluation of the program.

children.label: I
children.text: methods to link mammography and clinical breast examination records with population-based cancer registry data;

children.label: II
children.text: methods to provide diagnostic outcome data, or facilitate the communication of diagnostic outcome data, to radiology facilities for purposes of evaluating patterns of mammography interpretation; and

children.label: III
children.text: mechanisms for limiting access and maintaining confidentiality of all stored data; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to conduct pilot testing of the methods and mechanisms described in subclauses (I), (II), and (III) of clause (i) on a limited basis.

children.heading: Grant application
children.label: C Grant application
children.text: To be eligible to receive funds under this paragraph, an entity shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Report
children.label: D Report
children.text: A recipient of a grant under this paragraph shall submit a report to the Secretary containing the results of the study and testing conducted under clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (B), along with recommendations for methods of establishing a breast cancer screening surveillance system.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 2 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a breast cancer screening surveillance system based on the recommendations contained in the report described in paragraph (1)(D).

children.heading: Standards and procedures
children.label: 3 Standards and procedures
children.text: The Secretary shall establish standards and procedures for the operation of the breast cancer screening surveillance system, including procedures to maintain confidentiality of patient records.

children.heading: Information
children.label: 4 Information
children.text: The Secretary shall recruit facilities to provide to the breast cancer screening surveillance system relevant data that could help in the research of the causes, characteristics, and prevalence of, and potential treatments for, breast cancer and benign breast conditions, if the information may be disclosed under .section 552 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: to carry out, subject to paragraph (2), the certification program requirements under subsections (b), (c), (d), (g)(1), (h), (i), and (j) (including the requirements under regulations promulgated pursuant to such subsections), and

children.label: B
children.text: to implement the standards established by the Secretary under subsection (f),

children.label: A
children.text: the State has enacted laws and issued regulations relating to mammography facilities which are the requirements of this section (including the requirements under regulations promulgated pursuant to such subsections), and

children.label: i
children.text: has the legal authority and qualified personnel necessary to enforce the requirements of and the regulations promulgated pursuant to this section (including the requirements under regulations promulgated pursuant to such subsections),

children.label: ii
children.text: will devote adequate funds to the administration and enforcement of such requirements, and

children.label: iii
children.text: will provide the Secretary with such information and reports as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary shall carry out the Secretary’s functions under subsections (e) and (f);

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary may take action under subsections (h), (i), and (j); and

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary shall conduct oversight functions under subsections (g)(2) and (g)(3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may, after providing notice and opportunity for corrective action, withdraw the approval of a State’s authority under paragraph (1) if the Secretary determines that the State does not meet the requirements of such paragraph. The Secretary shall promulgate regulations for the implementation of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Effect of withdrawal
children.label: B Effect of withdrawal
children.text: If the Secretary withdraws the approval of a State under subparagraph (A), the certificate of any facility certified by the State shall continue in effect until the expiration of a reasonable period, as determined by the Secretary, for such facility to obtain certification by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, in accordance with this paragraph assess and collect fees from persons described in subsection (d)(1)(A) (other than persons who are governmental entities, as determined by the Secretary) to cover the costs of inspections conducted under subsection (g)(1) by the Secretary or a State acting under a delegation under subparagraph (A) of such subsection. Fees may be assessed and collected under this paragraph only in such manner as would result in an aggregate amount of fees collected during any fiscal year which equals the aggregate amount of costs for such fiscal year for inspections of facilities of such persons under subsection (g)(1). A person’s liability for fees shall be reasonably based on the proportion of the inspection costs which relate to such person.

children.heading: Deposit and availability
children.label: i Deposit and availability
children.text: Fees collected under subparagraph (A) shall be deposited as an offsetting collection to the appropriations for the Department of Health and Human Services as provided in appropriation Acts and shall remain available without fiscal year limitation.

children.heading: Appropriations
children.label: ii Appropriations
children.text: Fees collected under subparagraph (A) shall be collected and available only to the extent provided in advance in appropriation Acts.

children.label: A
children.text: to award research grants under subsection (p), such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 1993 through 2007; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the Secretary to carry out other activities which are not supported by fees authorized and collected under paragraph (1), such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 1993 through 2007.
